LAW AND MOTION 302, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR FAILURE TO EXHAUST PETITIONER'S ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDY. DESPITE GENERAL ALLEGATIONS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN IMPACTED BY NON-JET FUEL RELATED ENTITIES, THIS CASE REMAINS A CHALLENGE TO THE STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION ("BOE") REGULATION 1699(H) AND ITS IMPACT ON PETITIONER'S ABILITY TO RECOVER TAXES ON JET FUEL UTILIZIED IN SAN MATEO COUNTY. PREVIOUSLY, THE COURT GRANTED PETITIONER LEAVE TO AMEND TO IDENTIFY OTHER BUSINESS ENTITIES THAT ALLEGEDLY HAVE TAKEN UNFAIR ADVANTAGE OF THE REGULATION TO THE DETERMENT OF SAN MATEO COUNTY AND PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO DO SO. AS PREVIOUSLY NOTED, PETITIONER HAS A CLAIM PENDING BEFORE THE BOE TO RECOVER THE TAX REVENUES ALLEGEDLY DUE PETITIONER AND THAT CLAIM HAS NOT BEEN RULED UPON. AT THE SAME TIME, IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE LEGISLATURE HAS ENACTED LEGISLATION ON A PROSPECTIVE BASIS TO ADDRESS PETITIONER'S CONCERNS RELATED TO JET FUEL. GIVEN THE POSTURE OF THIS DISPUTE WITH THE CITY OF OAKLAND AND BOE, THE COURT DECLINES TO EXERCISE JURISDICTION OVER PETITIONER'S CLAIM. THE MOVING PAPERS OF BOE AND THE CITY OF OAKLAND ARE REPLETE WITH SOUND PUBLIC POLICY REASONS FOR THE COURT NOT TO INTERJECT ITSELF INTO THIS DISPUTE, AT LEAST UNTIL THE THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCESS BEFORE BOE IS COMPLETED. IN ADDITION, THE COURT SUSTAINED THE DEMURRER WITHOUT PREJUDICE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY OF OAKLAND'S DEMURRER (TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED THE FOLLOWING TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH WAS ISSUED PRIOR TO THE HEARING: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR FAILURE TO EXHAUST PETITIONER'S ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDY. DESPITE GENERAL ALLEGATIONS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN IMPACTED BY NON-JET FUEL RELATED ENTITIES, THIS CASE REMAINS A CHALLENGE TO THE STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION ("BOE") REGULATION 1699(H) AND ITS IMPACT ON PETITIONER'S ABILITY TO RECOVER TAXES ON JET FUEL UTILIZIED IN SAN MATEO COUNTY. PREVIOUSLY, THE COURT GRANTED PETITIONER LEAVE TO AMEND TO IDENTIFY OTHER BUSINESS ENTITIES THAT ALLEGEDLY HAVE TAKEN UNFAIR ADVANTAGE OF THE REGULATION TO THE DETERMENT OF SAN MATEO COUNTY AND PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO DO SO. AS PREVIOUSLY NOTED, PETITIONER HAS A CLAIM PENDING BEFORE THE BOE TO RECOVER THE TAX REVENUES ALLEGEDLY DUE PETITIONER AND THAT CLAIM HAS NOT BEEN RULED UPON. AT THE SAME TIME, IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE LEGISLATURE HAS ENACTED LEGISLATION ON A PROSPECTIVE BASIS TO ADDRESS PETITIONER'S CONCERNS RELATED TO JET FUEL. GIVEN THE POSTURE OF THIS DISPUTE WITH THE CITY OF OAKLAND AND BOE, THE COURT DECLINES TO EXERCISE JURISDICTION OVER PETITIONER'S CLAIM. THE MOVING PAPERS OF BOE AND THE CITY OF OAKLAND ARE REPLETE WITH SOUND PUBLIC POLICY REASONS FOR THE COURT NOT TO INTERJECT ITSELF INTO THIS DISPUTE, AT LEAST UNTIL THE THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCESS BEFORE BOE IS COMPLETED. IN ADDITION, THE COURT SUSTAINED THE DEMURRER WITHOUT PREJUDICE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Jan-23-2009 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION'S DEMURRERS, TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: David A. Silberman, Deputy County Counsel 650/363-4749 for pltf/petitioner & opposing party; Kristian D. Whitten, Deputy Atty General 703-5589 & Karen W. Yiu, Deputy Atty General 703-5385 for deft & moving party State Board Of Equalization; Rachel Wagner, Supervising Atty, Office of the City Atty 510/238-4921 for deft City of Oakland.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted the following tentative ruling, which was issued prior to the hearing: The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend for failure to exhaust petitioner's administrative remedy. Despite general allegations that plaintiff has been impacted by non-jet fuel related entities, this case remains a challenge to the State Board of Equalization ("BOE") Regulation 1699(h) and its impact on petitioner's ability to recover taxes on jet fuel utilizied in San Mateo County. Previously, the Court granted petitioner leave to amend to identify other business entities that allegedly have taken unfair advantage of the Regulation to the determent of San Mateo County and petitioner has failed to do so. As previously noted, petitioner has a claim pending before the BOE to recover the tax revenues allegedly due petitioner and that claim has not been ruled upon. At the same time, it is undisputed that the Legislature has enacted legislation on a prospective basis to address petitioner's concerns related to jet fuel. Given the posture of this dispute with the City of Oakland and BOE, the Court declines to exercise jurisdiction over petitioner's claim. The moving papers of BOE and the City of Oakland are replete with sound public policy reasons for the Court not to interject itself into this dispute, at least until the the administrative process before BOE is completed. In addition, the Court sustained the demurrer without prejudice. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
